subtasks.description: Decide keep/trim/remove; note constraint-based sizing adjustments.
completion_evidence: Optimization decisions for flagged tickers, quantitatively supported: (1) DGNX REMOVE—extreme volatility (ann vol >8), max drawdown -94%, only trace profitability, negative/low accruals, negative equity, no margins/ROE, little risk buffer, severe scenario losses. (2) FR REMOVE—very weak Sharpe (0.31), negative alpha, muted growth, declining returns, decelerating value creation, high price to cash-flow, all tool outputs below sector/peer medians. (3) IIPR REMOVE—negative alpha, barely positive returns, drawdown -66%, negative book/revenue growth, earnings contraction, tenant credit strain, even ‘safe’ REIT income underperforms; too much sector-specific/litigation risk. (4) TRNO REMOVE—Sharpe 0.15, negative alpha, high drawdown, weak profitability growth, decelerating cash flow, no offsetting risk factor. (5) STAG REMOVE—Sharpe 0.25, negative alpha, stagnant net asset growth, value underperformance, only moderate moat, not best-in-breed/diversifier. (6) EQIX REMOVE OR RADICAL DOWNSIZE—Sharpe only 0.21, negative alpha, very high price to book/cash flow, slowing value growth, risk/reward misaligned vs. alternatives. (7) CRDO TRIM—keep only small ‘thematic’ exposure if user strongly desires (AI call option), but risk cluster, very high volatility (beta >2.6), expensive, scenario losses >30%, valuation stretch. (8) ARM TRIM—growth moderating, negative recent press, beta >2.5, rapid drawdown potential; minor allocation only if user insists on AI/semis. (9) OKLO REMOVE—pre-revenue, huge valuation, only thematic conviction justifies, but negative returns/assets, negative accrual quality, not justified as core holding. All supported by systematic tool-derived data on risk, volatility, correlation, quality/growth scores, scenario stress, and recent news/fundamentals.

subtasks.description: Call add_task_evidence to record rationale/evidence for each decision.
completion_evidence: Rationale for each decision (tool-supported): DGNX: REMOVE—extreme volatility, catastrophic drawdowns, lack of positive cash flow or profitability, no risk buffers identified by performance/fundamental tools. FR, IIPR, TRNO, STAG: REMOVE—persistent poor Sharpe, negative or very low alpha, deteriorating growth/quality signals, scenario stress losses, underperform sector/peer medians. EQIX: REMOVE or RADICALLY DOWNSIZE—high valuation, weakening growth, negative alpha, lack of risk-adjusted return for a core holding. CRDO/ARM: TRIM—elevated risk cluster, sky-high beta, scenario loss >30–40%, decelerating quality/growth, appropriate only as satellites if user insists. OKLO: REMOVE—pre-revenue, very high risk, negative cash metrics, extremely speculative and not suitable for a core holding; only justified if user wants tiny lottery ticket. All decisions based on strong signals from returns/risk/fundamental data and news sentiment, not opinion.
